about.title: About LLMFit Web
about.description: Learn about our mission, how it works, and our data sources
about.mission_text: LLMFit Web aims to become the go-to reference platform for local LLM hardware compatibility. We help users quickly find the best open-source LLMs for their hardware without any technical barriers.
about.step1_text: Automatically detect your GPU model, VRAM, RAM, and CPU cores through browser APIs
about.step2_text: Match detected GPU to our database for complete hardware specs and performance tier
about.step3_text: Filter runnable models based on VRAM availability, excluding those beyond your hardware capability
about.step4_text: Score each model across four dimensions: quality, speed, compatibility, and context length
about.step5_text: Present ranked models best suited for your hardware, along with upgrade suggestions
about.data_sources_text: Model data comes from public benchmarks (MMLU, HumanEval, etc.), official technical reports, and community feedback. GPU specs are sourced from vendor documentation and published reviews. All data is regularly updated to maintain accuracy.
about.vision_text: We aim to cover 1000+ models and hundreds of GPU configurations, supporting 100K+ monthly active users. Future plans include more precise inference speed estimation, multi-GPU setup detection, and community rating systems.
contact.title: Contact Us
contact.description: Questions, suggestions, or feedback? We'd love to hear from you
contact.github_text: Feel free to submit Issues or PRs on GitHub to help improve this project
contact.response_text: We typically respond within 1-2 business days
contact.faq1_q: Do I need to install anything?
contact.faq1_a: No. LLMFit Web is a pure web tool. All hardware detection runs in your browser — no software installation required.
contact.faq2_q: Is my hardware information uploaded to your server?
contact.faq2_a: Hardware detection happens locally in your browser. Only when you explicitly trigger a recommendation request do anonymized hardware specs (GPU model, VRAM, etc.) get sent to our API for computation. We do not store any personally identifiable information.
contact.faq3_a: Our recommendations are based on real hardware specs and model requirements. The scoring algorithm considers quality, speed, compatibility, and context length. Actual performance may vary depending on driver version, inference framework, and other factors.
contact.faq4_q: How do I report incorrect model or GPU data?
contact.faq4_a: You can use the contact form on this page, send us an email, or submit an issue on GitHub. We greatly appreciate your feedback!
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: Learn how we collect, use, and protect your information
privacy.s1_text: LLMFit Web respects your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your information. By using this website, you agree to the practices described in this policy.
privacy.s2_hw_text: GPU model, VRAM size, RAM capacity, CPU cores, and OS type are sent to our server only when you explicitly trigger a recommendation request. This information is not linked to any personal identity and is used solely to generate recommendations.
privacy.s2_api_text: We log anonymous API call counts and error rates for service monitoring and performance optimization. No specific hardware configurations or personal data are recorded.
privacy.s2_usage_text: We use anonymous analytics (page views, visit duration, etc.) to understand site usage and improve our service.
privacy.s3_text: We use local storage to save your language preference and hardware configuration to enhance your experience. This data stays entirely in your browser and is never sent to our servers. We do not use tracking cookies or third-party advertising.
privacy.s4_text: We take reasonable technical measures to protect data in transit and at rest. API communication is encrypted via HTTPS. Since we do not collect personally identifiable information, even in the event of a data breach, your identity would not be exposed.
privacy.s5_text: Our recommendation API is hosted on Railway and Vercel. The database is hosted on Supabase. These service providers may collect limited data as described in their respective privacy policies.
privacy.s6_text: LLMFit Web is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.
privacy.s7_text: If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at:
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.description: By using LLMFit Web, you agree to the following terms and conditions
terms.s1_text: LLMFit Web provides LLM recommendation services based on hardware configuration. Recommendations are for reference only and do not constitute any form of performance guarantee. Actual model performance may vary due to driver versions, inference frameworks, system environments, and other factors.
terms.s2_text: This service is intended for developers, researchers, and enthusiasts interested in local LLM deployment. By using this service, you confirm that you are at least 13 years of age.
terms.s3_text1: The code, design, and content of the LLMFit Web website itself are copyrighted.
terms.s3_text2: Model names, GPU brands, and trademarks referenced on the site belong to their respective owners. LLMFit Web is not affiliated with NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, Apple, or any model developers.
terms.s4_text: As a user, you agree to: not abuse the API (including but not limited to high-frequency requests or automated scraping); not use this service to promote or recommend malicious content; comply with the open-source license terms of models you download and run.
terms.s5_text: This service is provided "as is" without any warranty of any kind, express or implied. We do not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or suitability of recommendations. LLMFit Web is not liable for hardware damage, data loss, or any other losses resulting from the use of recommended models.
terms.s6_text: We strive to maintain high availability but assume no liability for service interruptions, delays, or termination. We reserve the right to modify or discontinue the service at any time without prior notice.
terms.s7_text: Your use of the service is also governed by our Privacy Policy. Please refer to the Privacy Policy for more information.
terms.s8_text: If you have any questions or concerns about these Terms of Service, please contact us at:

seo.default_title: LLMFit Web — Find the Best Local LLMs for Your Hardware

reason.vram_headroom_high: Plenty of VRAM — can run at higher context lengths
reason.vram_tight_fit: Tight VRAM fit — consider a smaller quantization
reason.top_quality: Top-tier model quality for this hardware class
reason.excellent_speed: Excellent inference speed on your GPU
reason.perfect_match: Optimal VRAM match for your configuration
reason.great_context: Extra-long context window — great for large documents
scoring.title: How is the overall score calculated?
scoring.quality_desc: Evaluated from standard benchmark performance. Considers parameter count, architecture design, and community feedback.
scoring.speed_desc: Estimated tokens/sec on your GPU. Calculated from GPU compute power and model parameter count.
scoring.compatibility_desc: VRAM headroom analysis. More VRAM relative to model requirements = higher score.
scoring.context_desc: Context length evaluation. Longer context windows enable processing larger inputs.
